# Documentation Handling Standard

Owner: Mikhail Kurzin
Area: General
Approvers: Dmitry Kushnikov
Document ID: STD-GEN-01
Document Type: Standard
Document application: All Employees
Effective date: April 14, 2022
Reviewers: Mikhail Kurzin, Arijana Radic
Status: Published
Tag: ISO27001
Version: 2.1

<aside>

**Key points:**

- Documents maintenance
- Records retaining
- Documented Information Identification
</aside>

<aside>

*This document is developed to establish the basic rules of official documentation management within Manychat, describes steps needed to review and release a document and approach for records control. Document maintenance is mandatory to improve, update and/or officially release a document. The key goal of records retaining is to ensure obtaining of necessary, reliable information on processes performance.*

</aside>

---

# Table of Contents

---

# 1. General

## **1.1 Purpose**

The purpose of this document is to establish rules of documented information management for:

- approval of documents;
- update and review;
- ensuring the availability of current versions of relevant documents in the places of their use;
- ensuring the preservation of readability and fast identification of documents;
- documents accounting;
- control over the distribution of documents;
- version control;
- preventing accidental use of obsolete documents.

## **1.2 Scope**

This standard applies to all divisions, teams, and services of Manychat and all personnel of Manychat. It also applies to external organizations and their personnel who have been granted access to the Manychat infrastructure and services.

The document is reviewed at least annually to ensure it meets the current business requirements of Manychat, partners, customers, and external organizations.

# 2. Standard

## **2.1 General requirements**

Documents and records can be maintained electronically or on paper. The preferable method for maintaining documented information is electronic on Notion and Google Docs systems.

All official internal documents shall include:

- Type (Policy, Standard, Procedure, Record)
- Title
- Version
- Document Area
- Date of final approval
- List of approvers
- Description
- Confidentiality statement
- Document Owner
- Purpose and scope
- The content of the document (process description, description of applicable requirements implementation process, roles and responsibilities, etc.)
- Revision requirements (eg. annual)
- Revision control
- Compliance.

The documents must be approved by the working group on the criterion of their adequacy before publication.

Analysis and update of documents as required, or at least annually, as well as their re-approval.

Documented information hierarchy established in Manychat:

![Screenshot 2025-12-04 at 12.54.59 PM.png](Documentation%20Handling%20Standard/Screenshot_2025-12-04_at_12.54.59_PM.png)

[](data:image/png;base64,)

## **2.2 Documents and records identification**

There are below standards used for identification and management of documents and records within the Area:

| **Level** | **Type of document** | **Standard** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | Policies (intentions and direction of an organization expressed by Leadership team in specific Area) | POL-<Area code>-X-V |
| 2 | Standards (rules, requirements, fundamental concepts and principles in specific Area) | STD-<Area code>-X-V |
| 3 | Procedures (specify a way to carry out an activity or a process) | PR-<Area code>-X-V |
| 4 | Records, reports, plans, instructions etc. (evidences that support policies, standards and procedures activities) | REC-<Area code>-X-YYYY.MM-i |
- X – document or record sub-Area within the Area (optional);
- V – document unique ID;
- Y.M – date of record creation in format «YYYY».«MM» (optional);
- i – sequence number of records within each month.

These standards apply for all ManyChat documents and records.

The Company set the following common Area codes:

- GEN - General organization documents applied to all teams;
- IS - Information security;
- PR - Product area;
- DEV - Development;
- FIN - Finance;
- HR - Human resources;
- ACC - Accounting;
- INFRA - IT infrastructure;
- IT - Internal IT;
- LEGAL - Legal and compliance.

The key types of documented information include the following types of documents and means of their identification.

## **2.3 Creating and updating process**

When creating and updating documented information, the Company ensures appropriate:

- Identification and description (e.g. a title, date, author, or reference number);
- Format (e.g. language, software version, graphics) and media (e.g. paper, electronic); and
- Review and approval for suitability and adequacy.

The document must go through the following stages before official approval and distribution:

1. Prepare document for review
2. Organize review
3. Review document
4. Collect review comments
5. Discuss review comments
6. Update document
7. Obtain formal approval from designated approvers
8. Release document and all relevant parties notification.

*Reviewers* are involved to provide feedback, raise concerns, and stay informed about the document content. Reviewers’ input is advisory in nature and does not block document approval. Reviewers may explicitly indicate approval or provide no response if no objections are raised within the defined review period.

*Approvers* are responsible for formal approval of the document. Formal approval is mandatory and must be obtained in accordance with the document level and assigned sign-off roles.

The Company has established templates for policies, procedures, and standards ([**Policy Template](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ySl9Qt_snv-U-ay2WKEs6X1QGZHof1aj/edit), [Procedure/Standard Template](https://docs.google.com/document/d/19CLCcR6dSlBWY4k_hDdtrChniB0iDN0GBnaNR1AcSmU/edit#)**) that includes necessary identification, description, and format parameters for documented information created on its basis. The template is normally used for policies, procedures, and standards.

The Company utilizes cloud-based internet services (Notion and Google Docs) for creating and updating official documents.

Review and approval of documented information for its suitability and adequacy is made via the corporate channels like Slack and Emails that ensures proper communication routes and involvement of relevant managers.

There are different document review techniques of which the following three are most common:

- Distribution for comment: the author asks others for their feedback. This is a good means to inform others and get a reaction on the content of the document
- Peer review: the author asks colleagues or experts for their reaction on the content. The peer review is normally used in the concept phase of the creation of a document and stimulates the exchange of ideas.
- Formal review (approvers inspection): a structured process of trying to find defects in development documents. This method is used to formally approve a document.

Documents are developed by area or process owners. Organization of  document review is the responsibility of the document owner. If there is a need to update the document, the owner of the document updates it and notifies the employees about the newly published document. The approval of the updated version passes as for new documents. When a new version of the document is released, the previous one is considered to be invalid. If the document loses its relevance and is not needed for further use, it should be archived.

If documents must be signed by employees it should be done via document distribution system.

Documents that contain confidential information should be marked in accordance with [**Data Classification Policy**](https://www.notion.so/Data-Classification-Policy-2bfb12e9aa1a809aa48bd02294c10582?pvs=21).

Formal sign-off is required only from approvers and is performed in accordance with the document level as defined in the sign-off roles table:

| **Sign-off roles** | **Level of document** |
| --- | --- |
| Leadership team member | 1 - Policy |
| Leadership team member | 2 or 3 - Standard or Procedure |
| Depending on the document (Team Manager, Process Owner and etc.) | 4 - Records, Instructions, etc. |

## **2.4 Control of official documents**

The documented information is controlled to ensure:

- It is available and suitable for use, where and when it is needed; and
- It is adequately protected (e.g. from loss of confidentiality, improper use, or loss of integrity).

The policies, procedures, and standards should be published via cloud-based internet services (Notion and Google Drive) that make them available and suitable for use to respective employees whenever required.

The documented information must be protected according to its class arising from the information classification scheme (**Data Classification Policy**).

For the control of documented information, the Company addresses the following activities, as applicable:

- Distribution, access, retrieval, and use;
- Storage and preservation, including the preservation of legibility;
- Control of changes (e.g. version control);
- Retention and disposition.

Distribution, access, retrieval, and use of documented information are made upon the “need-to-know” basis.

Storage and preservation, including the preservation of legibility, are ensured by the information processing facilities and technologies utilized.

The sustainability of these facilities and technologies is ensured by the relevant terms of service supported by their providers.

Changes to the documented information are controlled over an automated versioning function, supported by the cloud-based internet services (Notion and Google Docs) upon authorized changes.

Only the latest versions of the documents are intended for users that are ensured by the cloud-based internet services (Notion and Google Docs) functions. Older (previous) versions are normally retained for information purposes (e.g. a user may compare current and previous versions of a document, using the corporate portal’s functionality to check what exactly has been changed).

Documented information not marked with versions (ticketing system, logs, emails, internal audit records, etc.) is of record nature and used as evidence of the relevant processes performed. A registered time stamp is required for extraction if records must be additionally protected from unauthorized release.

Each employee can print out the documentation from Notion and Google Drive and use it in the course of his work until the documentation loses its relevance.

## **2.5 Managing records**

Keeping and retaining records in the Manychat is carried out in order to obtain the necessary, reliable, and timely information regarding the productivity and effectiveness of the management and control.

Maintenance and storage of records are carried out electronically. Records, forms of references which are not formalized can be conducted in a free form.

Records should be protected in order to ensure their confidentiality, availability, and integrity, eliminate the possibility of misuse, unauthorized access. The measures include access management and backup of records.

The Process Owner is responsible for keeping the records. The Process owner can appoint those responsible for keeping and maintaining the records in working order.

Determination of the terms of storage of records is carried out in accordance with external and internal regulatory documents. For records storage period of which is not regulated, a storage period is established at least two years.

## **2.6 Documented information of external origin**

Documented information of external origin that is relevant to the Area  is recorded by the employee who receives the document in the registry of external documents (if necessary, to ensure traceability). If necessary, information security measures (the same as for ISMS records) are applied to documented information of external origin.

Documented information of external origin, determined by the Company as being necessary for planning and operation of the ISMS, is identified as appropriate and controlled. Such information includes applicable legislation, by-laws, regulatory documents, standards and so forth.

The ISMS policies, procedures, and standards contain normative references, pointing to the relevant documented information of external origin applicable and used within the ISMS.

# 3. Roles and Responsibilities

The CEO has the overall responsibility for Information Security at the Company, including information security regarding documents.

Process specific roles and responsibilities:

| **Role** | **Role description** |
| --- | --- |
| **All employees and contractors** | Take responsible actions to comply with the standard. |
| **Managers** | Approval of documentation and records in their area of  responsibility. |
| **Document/records owners** | Documents and records development. |
| **Legal** | Agreement on key regulatory documents and records;
Approval of legal nature documentation and records. |

# 4. Compliance

The proof of ISMS quality is the receipt and maintenance of the certificate of ISO/IEC 27001:2022 «Information Technology. Security techniques. Information security management systems. Requirements» compliance.

Manychat must comply with multiple state regulatory requirements of countries, Manychat operates in, such as:

- The General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) (Regulation (EU) 2016/679);
- Personal data protection regulation in the countries or regions of operation (eg. CCPA);
- Canada’s Anti-Spam Law (or “CASL”);
- The CAN-SPAM Act;
- other mandatory state regulatory acts including acts on commercial secrets and intellectual property.

## **4.1 Enforcement**

Implementation and enforcement of this policy is ultimately the responsibility of all employees at Manychat. Security team may conduct ad-hoc checks to ensure compliance with this document without notice. Any violation shall require immediate corrective action. Violations shall be noted in the Manychat applications and responsible employees shall be dispatched to remediate the issue.

For Manychat employees, breaches of this policy may result in disciplinary action being taken and will be dealt with under the disciplinary process in accordance with state laws and regulations of the residence country. Any serious breaches or repeated breaches of this policy may be deemed by Manychat as gross misconduct and may result in disciplinary sanctions, including a remark, reprimand, and dismissal from work.

## **4.2 Exceptions**

Exceptions to this standard must be processed in accordance with the deviation handling rules by the Security team. All exceptions must be maintained taking into account associated risks, exceptions, and recorded approvals. Continuously detected deviations may trigger the changes or improvements of the measures defined in this document.
